chunks.title: Course Information, Catalog Description, and Special Course Designation
chunks.content:
Meeting Times: Meeting Type: LEC
Meeting Days: TR
Start Time: 12:45PM
End Time: 2:00PM
Start Date: 01/12/2026
End Date: 05/05/2026
Meeting Location: ZACH 310
Meeting Times: Meeting Type: LAB
Meeting Days: F
Start Time: 3:00PM
End Time: 5:00PM
Start Date: 01/12/2026
End Date: 05/05/2026
Meeting Location: EABA 118
Credit Hours: 3

Software Reverse Engineering. (2-2). Credit 3. Overview of the compilation
mechanism to generate executable files and raw binary codes from source codes;
executable file formats for an operating system to run the binary code; disassembly
algorithms and control graph analysis; static and dynamic analyses; case studies on
code obfuscation, codebreaking, malware analysis. Prerequisite: Grade of C or
better in CSCE 313 or approval of instructor.

Special Course Designation
None

chunks.title: Course Prerequisites
chunks.content:
Prerequisite/Corequisite(s): Grade of C or better in CSCE 313 or approval of
instructor.

chunks.title: Course Learning Outcomes
chunks.content:
Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:

• Analyze simple assembly programs to determine their functionality.
• Use a debugger to find code that has been packed or encrypted.
• Perform dynamic analysis to detect program behavior.
• Analyze malware safely in a contained environment.
• Understand process injection.
• Reverse engineer programs containing simple anti-disassembly and anti-debugging techniques.

chunks.title: Textbook and/or Resource Materials
chunks.content:
This material Is: Required
Practical Malware Analysis
ISBN: 9781593272906
Authors: Michael Sikorski, Andrew Honig
Publisher: No Starch Press
Publication Date: 2012-02-01
URL for Resource:
https://ebookcentral.proquest.com/lib/tamucs/detail.action
?docID=1137570
Notes:
Thanks to the support of Texas A&M University Libraries,
you can access this course material for free at the URL.

chunks.title: Additional Instructional Materials
chunks.content:
This Material is: Required
Laptop
Notes:
Students will need to bring a laptop to class. That laptop should be able to run Virtual
Box and have 50GB of hard drive space available for virtual machines.

chunks.title: Grading Policy
chunks.content:
The grading scale will be:
A: grade>=90.0
B: 80.0<=grade<90.0
C: 70.0<=grade<80.0
D: 60.0<=grade<70.0
F: grade<60

The overall grade will be determined as follows:
Homework 60%
Final Project 30%
Quizzes 10%

Students enrolled in CSCE 652 are expected to have a more substantive final project
(e.g. involving creation of reusable tools, or a more difficult malware sample).

chunks.title: Late Work Policy
chunks.content:
Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not
considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy (Student Rule 7).

chunks.title: Course Specific Late Work Policy
chunks.content:
Homework challenges may be submitted late until the CTF scoreboard closes for half
credit. Neither quizzes nor the final project may be submitted late.

chunks.title: AI Statement
chunks.content:
This course assumes that all work submitted by students will be generated by the
students themselves. Students should not have another person/entity do the writing of
any substantive portion of an assignment for them, which includes hiring a person or a
company to write assignments and using artificial intelligence tools like ChatGPT.
